title: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The smallest unit of work effort consuming both time and resources that a project manager can schedule and control; a project step that takes time and uses resources.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The maximum number of days an activity can be delayed without delaying the entire project; computed as LS − ES or LF − EF; zero for critical activities.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The procedure of moving right to left through the network diagram to calculate Latest Finish and Latest Start for every activity, beginning from the project end date.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The deliberate reduction of an activity's duration by adding extra resources or cost to shorten the critical path and reduce total project duration; only effective on critical activities.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: Any activity that lies on the critical path; it has zero slack and any delay to it directly delays the entire project by the same amount.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The longest path from Start to Finish in a network diagram; determines the minimum possible project duration; activities on it have zero slack.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: A project scheduling technique that identifies all paths through a network diagram, determines the critical path, and computes earliest/latest start and finish times and slack for every activity.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The estimated time required to complete one activity, measured in consistent units; the value shown in each node of the network diagram.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The earliest possible day an activity can be completed, calculated as ES + Duration in the forward pass through the network.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The earliest possible day an activity can begin, equal to the maximum Earliest Finish among all predecessors; Day 0 for activities with no predecessors.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: Another term for slack — the amount of scheduling flexibility available to an activity or path without delaying the project end date.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The procedure of moving left to right through the network diagram to calculate Earliest Start and Earliest Finish for every activity, beginning from the project start.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The latest day an activity can be completed without delaying the project; equal to the minimum Latest Start of all immediate successors; calculated in the backward pass.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The latest day an activity can begin without delaying the project; calculated as LF − Duration in the backward pass.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: A directed graph representing all project activities and their precedence relationships; nodes are activities and arrows are dependencies; used to identify all paths and the critical path.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: A connecting point in a network diagram; in Activity-on-Node format, each node represents one activity and displays the activity label and duration.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: A sequence of activities leading continuously from the starting node to the finishing node of a network diagram; its length is the sum of its activities' durations.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The difference between the critical path length and a given path's length; represents how much total delay that path can absorb before it becomes the new critical path.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: An activity that must be fully completed before a given activity can begin; shown in the predecessor column of an activity table and as an incoming arrow in a network diagram.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems

cards.definition: The total time required to complete the entire project; equal to the length of the critical path; cannot be reduced without crashing one or more critical activities.
cards.sourceLecture: Practice Problems: Project Management Practice Problems
